question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user return a nodesEnvelope with a very large Nodes array (millions of entries) from a malicious mirror REST server, causing the for loop over payload.Nodes to consume excessive CPU and memory while building the network map, delaying or preventing TransferTransaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that os.WriteFile('/tmp/alive', ...) uses mode 0644 (world-readable), allowing any co-tenant process in the container to read the heartbeat timestamp and infer the exact timing of TransferTransaction submission cycles to perform targeted transaction front-running?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user control a malicious consensus node (via poisoned network map) to return a TRANSACTION_EXPIRED receipt for every submitted TransferTransaction, causing the retry loop to exhaust all cfg.maxRetries attempts and log 'all attempts failed', effectively blocking all legitimate transfers?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to '0s', which fails the cfg.baseBackoff <= 0 check and is silently replaced with 1s, but set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a large value, causing rapid-fire retry loops that submit duplicate TransferTransactions to the network?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry logic in buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() by returning HTTP 429 or 5xx responses from a malicious mirror REST server, causing the pinger to retry indefinitely (up to mirrorNodeClientMaxRetries) and never establish a valid network map, permanently blocking TransferTransaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to 'other' and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an SSRF target (e.g. 'http://169.254.169.254/'), causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to make HTTP requests to internal cloud metadata endpoints and potentially leak credentials used to sign TransferTransactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a serviceEndpoint with port 0, which is skipped by the 'ep.Port == 0' check, and simultaneously inject a second endpoint with a valid port pointing to an attacker-controlled server, ensuring all TransferTransactions are routed to the attacker while the legitimate node is silently excluded?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a transaction memo or unique nonce in hiero.NewTransferTransaction() to make submitted transactions indistinguishable from replayed ones, preventing the pinger from detecting that its transactions are being replayed by an attacker?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a very large duration (e.g. '8760h'), causing time.NewTicker(cfg.interval) to fire only once per year, effectively disabling the pinger's transaction submission and masking any tampering with the Hedera network that the pinger is meant to detect?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID to a non-existent or attacker-controlled account ID that passes hiero.AccountIDFromString() validation, causing client.SetOperator() to use an invalid operator so that all TransferTransactions are rejected by the network with INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user return a nodesEnvelope JSON with a GrpcProxyEndpoint pointing to an attacker-controlled proxy, and if the SDK uses grpc_proxy_endpoint for transaction routing, cause all TransferTransactions to be proxied through the attacker's server where they can be inspected or modified?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the TransferTransaction is constructed with only two AddHbarTransfer calls (operator debit, toID credit) and no explicit node ID, allowing the SDK to automatically select a node from the network map that may have been poisoned by an attacker via a malicious mirror REST response?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the log.Printf output of 'transfer success: status=%s txID=%s elapsed=%s' to harvest transaction IDs from container logs, then use those IDs to query the Hedera network for transaction details and infer the operator account's transfer patterns for targeted attacks?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large integer, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to retry indefinitely against a slow mirror REST server, blocking the newClient() call and preventing the pinger from ever submitting TransferTransactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user return a JSON response from a malicious mirror REST server where NodeAccountID values are valid strings but map to attacker-controlled Hedera accounts, causing hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap) to associate attacker account IDs with attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints and route all TransferTransactions accordingly?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: newClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'other' network path where buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() is called with context.Background() (no deadline), allowing an attacker-controlled mirror REST server to hold the connection open indefinitely and prevent the pinger from initializing, blocking all TransferTransaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cryptoTransfer.Execute(client) does not set an explicit transaction valid duration, relying on SDK defaults, so that an attacker who delays transaction delivery (via a malicious node) can replay the transaction within the default validity window?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 63 or higher, causing backoff() to compute base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1)) where 1<<62 overflows int64, producing a negative or zero sleep duration that causes the retry loop to spin without delay and flood the network with TransferTransaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a serviceEndpoint with an IPAddressV4 value of '0.0.0.0' or '127.0.0.1', causing net.JoinHostPort() to build a loopback or wildcard address that the SDK connects to locally, redirecting TransferTransaction submissions to a local attacker-controlled process?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the pinger writes /tmp/ready after newClient() succeeds but before the first TransferTransaction is submitted, creating a TOCTOU window where the readiness probe reports healthy but no transactions are actually being sent?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to a syntactically valid but semantically wrong key type (e.g. an ECDSA key instead of ED25519), causing hiero.PrivateKeyFromString() to succeed but produce signatures that are rejected by the Hedera network, causing all TransferTransactions to fail with INVALID_SIGNATURE?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS certificate validation in the httpClient (no custom TLS config) to perform a TLS stripping or certificate substitution attack on the HTTPS connection to the mirror REST server, injecting a malicious nodesEnvelope that redirects TransferTransactions to attacker-controlled nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that resp.GetReceipt(client) polls the same (potentially attacker-controlled) consensus node for the receipt, allowing the attacker to return a forged SUCCESS receipt for a transaction that was silently dropped, causing the pinger to report false transfer success?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the goroutine that writes to /tmp/alive every 15 seconds by creating a symlink at /tmp/alive pointing to a sensitive file, causing os.WriteFile('/tmp/alive', ...) to overwrite that file with a timestamp, leading to data corruption or privilege escalation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK to a value with leading/trailing whitespace that survives strings.TrimSpace() in envOr() but fails the switch statement in newClient(), causing a 'unknown network' error that prevents client initialization and blocks all TransferTransaction submissions?
